title: The Ghosthunters
australian_relation: ABC Radio National transcript naming Picton ghost tours and Woodford Academy as haunted public places.
coordinate_evidence_note: Public page text names `Picton` in a ghost/supernatural context; gazetteer result state-checked as Picton, New South Wales, 2571, Australia.
evidence_summary: 1:30pmWed 9 Mar 2005 at 1:30pm Wed 9 Mar 2005 at 1:30pm Sorry, this audio is not yet available or has expired Brought to you by ABC Radio National Presented by Claudia Taranto Across Australia interest in the paranormal is growing, there are more than 40 ghost tours on offer and some people even sleep overnight in old gaols and hospitals in order to have close contact with the spirit world. Ghosthunters, or Paranormal Investigators, are interested in finding subjective, scientific evidence of ghosts. They spend their weekends visiting reputedly haunted sites and wiring them up with cameras, video and sound recorders, electro magnetic field meters and temperature gauges. In the program we meet some ghosthunters and hear their evidence, we also join a ghost tour in Picton NSW and eavesdrop on an investigation of Woodford Academy, a haunted National Trust property in the Blue Mountains..
